text:
Team GB star Josh Kerr has revealed he went deaf during theOlympics1500 metre final on Tuesday.The Scot clinched a silver medalafter being pipped at the post by USA star Cole Hocker.

The 1500m final had been billed as a battle between Kerr and his nemesis Jakob Ingebrigtsen ahead of the race.

And Ingebrigtsen led from the off and he ran at pace in an attempt to storm clear of the field.

The chasing pack were able to stay in the race, however, with Kerr making a dart to the front on the final bend.

But Hocker surged up the inside from third place to cross the finish line in first. And Ingebrigtsen missed out on a medal altogether as American Yared Nuguse made a late rush.

Kerr spoke toHeartradio to give his thoughts on the race on Wednesday morning. And the 26-year-old explained: “So the thing yesterday - and it’s something I deal with quite a lot post-race, but it’s never happened mid race - my hearing just goes.

Read more:Olympic athlete arrested 'on suspicion of trying to buy cocaine in Paris'



Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

“We’re obviously pushing through some walls pretty hard. And so normally my eyesight or my hearing goes after the race, but 400 meters in, it was so loud that my hearing just went and I was like, ‘I’ve got to focus up here’. It was bouncing in there, it was electric.

“It kicks you on, but you’re so focused in on the moment, you’re letting the crowd bear the energy when you’re falling apart a little bit.

“That last 100 was a long 100 meters for me, but I was nearly a bronze medallist by a point of one [of a second], so the crowd brought me the whole way home.”

Don't miss:Team GB star goes back to work this week after missing out on medal in Paris

Don't miss:Team GB athlete pushes rival after four runners wiped out in 5,000m race

Kerr overcame Ingebrigtsen to clinch the gold medal in the 1500m at the World Championship in Budapest a year ago after making a late push to the front.

He previously missed out to the Norwegian in the 1500m final at the Tokyo Games three years ago as the Briton clinched an Olympics bronze medal, with Kenyan ace Kenyan Cheruiyot grabbing the silver.

And Kerr admitted that he has lost the medal he won in Japan. “I’m not going to lie, I have absolutely no idea where the Tokyo medal is, he added. “That might be gone, for the ages. I genuinely have no idea. What I found is, if you lose one, you get a better colour of the next one.”

title: Team GB star went deaf mid-race after pushing body to limit for Olympic medal | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Tom Parsons
description: An Olympic medallist has admitted his hearing went during his race in Paris.
